clusters.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
clusters.neutral_headline: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
bullet_summary: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday
Reported by Al Jazeera, BBC World, NPR News and others

items.title: Jason Collins, first openly gay active NBA player, dies after cancer battle
items.standfirst: The 213cm centre, who publicly came out as gay in 2013, had been battling a brain tumour.
items.content: The 213cm centre, who publicly came out as gay in 2013, had been battling a brain tumour.

items.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
items.standfirst: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.
items.content: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.

items.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor
items.standfirst: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.
items.content: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)

items.title: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 from brain cancer

items.content: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player, who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died, his family announced Tuesday.

items.title: Trump-Xi summit: Trade, Taiwan and Iran on the table at high-stakes Beijing meeting
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump's highly anticipated visit to Beijing on Wednesday comes at a restless moment for a world worried about war and trade disputes. Trump has said he will have a "long talk" about Iran with Chinese leader Xi Jinping and will also discuss US arms sales to Taiwan. Here's what to know about the summit.

items.standfirst: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.
items.content: President Donald Trump will begin a two-day summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing on Thursday as the two countries engage in a fierce trade war and the U.S. conflict with Iran rages on.

items.title: Middle East live: Israel strikes cars on Lebanese highway south of Beirut
items.standfirst: Israeli strikes targeted two cars on Wednesday on a major highway linking Beirut to southern Lebanon, state media reported, a day after 13 people were killed in attacks on towns in the south. Earlier, US President Donald ⁠Trump said he expected to have a "long talk" about the Iran war with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping when they meet for a high-stakes summit in Beijing. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: What's Trump's game plan in China amid Iran war?
items.standfirst: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.
items.content: President Trump headed to China on Tuesday for a high-stakes and highly anticipated summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Before leaving, Mr. Trump downplayed the need for Xi to intervene in the stalled peace talks with Iran. Anna Coren reports from Beijing.

items.title: Trump heads to high-stakes summit in China
items.standfirst: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.
items.content: President Trump has left for Beijing and a high-stakes summit with China’s President Xi. NBC Nightly News anchor Tom Llamas is there.

items.title: Trump 'could choose to change Taiwan policy in Beijing' unhindered by Congress, analyst says
items.standfirst: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, David Sacks, Fellow for Asia studies at the Council on Foreign Relations, explains that Trump "could choose to change US policy towards Taiwan and Congress only has so many things that it can do to try to rein that in". U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese ​President Xi ‌Jinping are holding two days of meetings on Thursday and ⁠Friday where the issue of Taiwan, which Beijing views as its own territory, is certain to come up.
items.content: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, David Sacks, Fellow for Asia studies at the Council on Foreign Relations, explains that Trump "could choose to change US policy towards Taiwan and Congress only has so many things that it can do to try to rein that in". clusters.title: Japanese crisp bags turn black and white due to Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
bullet_summary: Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May
Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Japanese crisp bags turn black and white due to Iran war
items.standfirst: Global oil prices rose further on Tuesday with Brent crude climbing near $110 a barrel, as a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz continues with the US and Iran both rejecting the other's ceasefire proposal. Meanwhile, Japanese snack giant Calbee has decided to temporarily switch to black and white packaging for some of its products, including crisps and prawn crackers. This is due to instability in the supply of printing ink, which is made using petrochemical materials.
items.content: Global oil prices rose further on Tuesday with Brent crude climbing near $110 a barrel, as a standoff over the Strait of Hormuz continues with the US and Iran both rejecting the other's ceasefire proposal. Meanwhile, Japanese snack giant Calbee has decided to temporarily switch to black and white packaging for some of its products, including crisps and prawn crackers. This is due to instability in the supply of printing ink, which is made using petrochemical materials.

items.title: Snack giant switches to black and white packaging as Iran war hits ink supplies
items.standfirst: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.
items.content: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely disrupted global supplies of energy and petrochemicals.

items.title: Iran war oil shortage forces Japan snack giant to use black-and-white packaging
items.standfirst: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. Continue reading...
items.content: Calbee to switch its brightly coloured packaging to black and white because war has disrupted supply of certain raw materials used in inkJapan’s biggest snack maker has been forced to use black-and-white packaging for some flagship products because of ink ingredient shortages caused by the strait of Hormuz blockade.Calbee, whose potato chip brands in particular are known for brightly coloured bag designs, said 14 of its products would switch to monochrome branding by the end of May. clusters.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
clusters.neutral_headline: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
bullet_summary: The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed

items.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
items.standfirst: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
items.content: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.(Image credit: Michel Euler)

items.title: French woman battling hantavirus is critically ill and on an artificial lung
items.standfirst: A French woman repatriated from the cruise ship struck by hantavirus is in intensive care and being treated with an artificial lung, doctors at a Paris hospital said Tuesday as the country's health minister said there was "no evidence" of widespread circulation of the rare virus.
items.content: A French woman repatriated from the cruise ship struck by hantavirus is in intensive care and being treated with an artificial lung, doctors at a Paris hospital said Tuesday as the country's health minister said there was "no evidence" of widespread circulation of the rare virus.
